concept: THE REFEREE ARRIVES: Alaska's bitter salmon bycatch argument finally gets a machine that can count what nobody could agree on, and the count that matters most is the one the Sitka Tribe of Alaska built for its own river.

angle.thesis: AI is not going to bring the salmon back, but it is handing Alaskans a faster, harder-to-argue-with count of what is actually in the net and on the river, and the most consequential version is the Sitka Tribe of Alaska building that count for its own subsistence fishery instead of waiting on anyone else's numbers.
supporting_facts: Sitka Tribe of Alaska: $200,000 USFWS Tribal Wildlife Grant (Oct 16, 2024), 'Artificial Intelligence for Subsistence Salmon Monitoring and Management', Redoubt Lake

NOAA AFSC customized YOLOv11: 97% of fish detected, 82% prediction accuracy (per NOAA); 85% fewer frames need human review, days-or-weeks becomes hours (per National Fisherman); research, not deployed
Sullivan Bycatch Reduction and Research Act: introduced Jan 7 2026, revamped version reported July 17 2026 (KUCB)
angle.fair_counterpoint: NOAA's model is research showing promise, not deployed on deck; Western Alaska declines are driven substantially by ocean and climate conditions; bycatch is incidental and capped. The referee does not rescue the fish.
angle.runner_ups: Measurement Layer (killed: unsupported legal-trigger claim), Who Controls the Count (untellable muted, survives as subtext), Measurement Is Not a Cure (doom valence, survives as the one-line bound)

treatment.judge_reasoning: Won on emotional arc (9) and fairness (9): the boom-up reveal of the referee on a dry gravel bar IS the thesis as one frame. Grafts: Explainer's recurring caption question (open hook + button resolve) and RESEARCH stamp and fogged-lens loop image; Comedian's pollock-stack scale gag; Enthusiast's she-built-it authorship beat and raven open-to-close tally match cut. ScanReticle dropped entirely per Gate 0D: the count is a mechanical TallyCounter.
treatment.recurring_gag: the Raven keeping its own honest tally, open to close
treatment.signature_shot: boom-up pull-back: referee ankle-deep on a dry gravel bar, a true count over a near-empty river (at most 3 fish visible at widest)

fingerprint.palette: bone-tan gravel, milky glacial jade, dawn cream amber, sockeye crimson accent, counter-brass and dial-cream
fingerprint.metaphor: the bitter salmon argument finally gets a referee: a counting clicker that settles numbers no one could agree on, and a tribal weir that brings the count home

palette: bone-tan gravel, milky glacial jade, dawn cream amber, sockeye crimson accent, counter-brass and dial-cream

light.sun: low dawn sun from screen-right, long raking shadows across the gravel bars
light.mood: exposed honest morning light: the river laid bare is the argument laid bare

divergence_note: The last two dispatches were night worlds: 07-19 a cold indigo powerplant vigil (eye-level, centered hero, push-in only), 07-20 an orbital-aerial thermal-IR drone piece over a fire range (radial emanate, instrument readout). This board is a DAY diorama: an eye-level two-bank river argument in bone-tan and jade, horizontal traverse of crowds and fish, a multiplicity-swarm ensemble instead of a single machine hero, and a rise-reveal signature crane instead of a descent or orbit. The count mark is a mechanical cream-and-brass TallyCounter, deliberately not the ScanReticle HUD language of 07-20 and not the mint count overlays of 07-08/07-11. Palette shares zero tokens with either of the last two runs.

draw.subject: chrome salmon nose against a fogged underwater lens
draw.action: noses up to the glass as a cream odometer digit flips to 0001

beats.means: the question the whole piece answers, asked as a picture
beats.shows: the fogged lens, the salmon nose, the flipping cream digit

choreo.primary: the odometer digit flips to 0001 exactly as the question lands
choreo.reaction: the salmon flinches back a half-body, fins flare, then settles closer
choreo.ambient: silt motes drift, condensation drips crawl the glass, water caustics shimmer

draw.subject: two crowds on opposite gravel banks (subsistence family left, pollock crew right)
draw.action: shout jagged speech shards past each other over one thin braided channel

beats.means: the argument is loud and nobody is looking at the fish
beats.shows: the two-bank river diorama and both shouting crowds

choreo.primary: camera pulls wide as the two crowds lean in and shout past each other
choreo.reaction: speech shards collide mid-river and shatter into splinters that rain on the water
choreo.ambient: channel current slides, far ridge parallax drifts, clothing flaps in the wind

draw.subject: two opposing StatCards with mismatched totals
draw.action: slam in tilted against each other while a raven plucks the decimal point off one and flaps away
draw.emotion: false vindication on the card owner's face

beats.means: a decade of numbers nobody trusts, made physical
beats.shows: the dueling mismatched StatCards and the decimal-thief raven

choreo.primary: two StatCards slam in tilted against each other with overshoot
choreo.reaction: the raven's pluck makes the total balloon 10x and the card owner puffs up proudly
choreo.ambient: cards wobble on their spikes, raven feathers ruffle, crowd fists keep pumping

draw.subject: SMOKEHOUSE swing sign over bare drying racks, worried family beneath
draw.action: sign creaks in the wind over empty racks
draw.annotation: Yukon kings: 'a prolonged period of low productivity' (ADFG)

beats.means: the human cost upriver, played straight
beats.shows: the SMOKEHOUSE sign, the bare racks, the worried family

choreo.primary: the swing sign creaks to a stop over the empty racks as the line lands
choreo.reaction: the family's shoulders drop together, a child looks up at the parent
choreo.ambient: wind ripples grass tufts, loose twine sways from the racks, low mist slides

draw.subject: pollock crewman on the heaving deck
draw.action: shakes a lone salmon out of the net so eagerly it splashes his neighbor

beats.means: the fleet's fair point drawn fair: they do not want this fish
beats.shows: the fleet's deck and the flung salmon

choreo.primary: the crewman shakes the salmon out of the net in a motion-blurred fling
choreo.reaction: the splash soaks his neighbor who recoils and shakes off spray
choreo.ambient: the boat heaves on the swell, rigging lines sway, gulls wheel far back

draw.subject: a cold ocean swell as a cross-section
draw.action: a visibly cold current mass physically shoves the red run-curve downward; the boat above tips and rights itself
draw.annotation: ocean and climate drive much of the decline

beats.means: the honest driver nobody can regulate
beats.shows: the ocean cross-section pressing the run-curve down

choreo.primary: the cold current mass shoves the run-curve down in one physical push
choreo.reaction: the boat above tips and rights itself as the curve bottoms out
choreo.ambient: cross-section bubbles rise, current bands slide at different depths

draw.subject: buried human observer at a light table
draw.action: drowns in a paper storm of net-cam frames while calendar pages tear off reading DAYS then WEEKS

beats.means: the old bottleneck: human review at human speed
beats.shows: the buried observer, the paper storm, the shedding calendar

choreo.primary: calendar pages tear off DAYS then WEEKS and join the paper storm
choreo.reaction: the observer sinks deeper with each page, pencil drooping until it drops
choreo.ambient: papers flutter continuously, the desk lamp flickers, dust hangs in the beam

draw.subject: frame-stacks as towers
draw.action: pollock stack builds through the ceiling while a modest salmon pile waves a tiny STILL HERE flag
draw.emotion: the salmon pile sheepish, the clock smug
draw.annotation: the training pile: every fish hand-labeled

beats.means: scale of the training data as a picture; the numeral lands when it is spoken (28.5)
beats.shows: the pollock tower and the salmon pile with its flag

choreo.primary: the pollock frame-tower builds through the ceiling in stacked slams
choreo.reaction: each slam shakes the room 2px and the little salmon pile waves its flag harder
choreo.ambient: loose frames vibrate, ceiling dust falls, the brass head's sweep-light strafes

draw.subject: the brass counting head and a wall clock
draw.action: sweeps the whole frame wall in one strafing pass as the clock spins and lands on HOURS
draw.emotion: the clock smug, the observer blinking awake
draw.annotation: days or weeks becomes HOURS (per National Fisherman)

beats.means: the speed win, given its own breath
beats.shows: the sweep pass and the clock landing on HOURS

choreo.primary: the brass head strafes the wall in one pass and the clock lands on HOURS with a ding
choreo.reaction: reviewed frames flip green in the sweep's wake; the observer sits up
choreo.ambient: paper flakes settle, lamp glow steadies, dust drifts

beats.rehook: the machine out-counts everyone in the room and gets stamped STILL RESEARCH in the same breath

draw.subject: pollock and red-kype sockeye in a police lineup
draw.action: a brass clicker wheel ratchets as it tallies the sockeye; a big stamp thuds STILL RESEARCH over the scorecard
draw.emotion: pollock sweating one bead, sockeye deadpan
draw.annotation: 85,000+ fish labels; 97% spotted, 82% accuracy (per NOAA AFSC)

beats.sfx: wheel ratchet, hard metal die stamp
beats.means: capability and honesty in one frame; both spoken numbers drawn at their moment
beats.shows: the fish lineup, the ratcheting clicker wheel, the STILL RESEARCH stamp

choreo.primary: the 85,000+ chip latches onto the clicker's first tally tick exactly as the words land, then the 97% scorecard follows
choreo.reaction: the pollock sweats a single bead and leans away; the stamp thud bounces both fish
choreo.ambient: lineup height-lines shimmer, wheel teeth glint, stamp ink mist settles

draw.subject: a Senate bill document with a trembling pen
draw.action: pen hovers, a stamp lands TRANSPARENCY on the page
draw.annotation: Bycatch Reduction and Research Act, revamped July 17 (KUCB)

beats.sfx: paper rustle, pen scratch, wet ink pad press
beats.means: the in-window peg: the law now demands trusted numbers
beats.shows: the bill document, the trembling pen, the TRANSPARENCY stamp

choreo.primary: the TRANSPARENCY stamp lands on the page with a squash and settle
choreo.reaction: the trembling pen jumps at the thud and hovers again, still unsigned
choreo.ambient: page corners lift in a draft, dust motes cross the lamp light

draw.subject: a referee in a striped vest
draw.action: steps to mid-river, inflates chest, blows the whistle, raises a clicker that spins and locks on a clean total; both crowds lower their signs
draw.annotation: one count both sides can read (STILL RESEARCH tag on the clicker)

beats.sfx: whistle blast, clicker zip-lock, sudden hush
beats.means: the fight finally has a referee
beats.shows: the referee mid-river, the whistle, the raised locking clicker

choreo.primary: the referee blows the whistle and the raised clicker spins then locks
choreo.reaction: both crowds freeze mid-shout and lower their signs in a staggered cascade; a small STILL RESEARCH tag swings from the clicker
choreo.ambient: river current keeps sliding, vest stripes flutter, whistle lanyard sways

draw.subject: the whole river valley from above, rising
draw.action: camera booms up and pulls back until the referee is tiny on a dry gravel bar, at most 3 fish visible in the entire channel
draw.annotation: a true count. an almost empty river.

beats.means: the thesis as one frame: referees settle arguments, they do not refill rivers
beats.shows: the rising wide: the tiny referee, the gravel ribcage, the near-empty channel

choreo.primary: the camera booms up and pulls back in one composed rise
choreo.reaction: a single lone fish crosses the empty channel under the hold; a cloud shadow rakes across the gravel
choreo.ambient: atmosphere haze deepens with altitude, water glints crawl, shadows rake long

draw.subject: a biologist in a Sitka Tribe of Alaska vest at Redoubt Lake
draw.action: plants a timber video weir into the gravel with both hands; the counter chip anchors to HER stake

beats.means: she did not receive the machine, she built it
beats.shows: the Sitka biologist planting the timber weir, the anchoring counter chip

choreo.primary: an empty stake hole and her hands enter frame first (0.4s telegraph), then she drives the weir stake home with both hands, squash then settle
choreo.reaction: the counter chip anchors to the stake and its housing glints as water ripples out
choreo.ambient: lake shimmer band, spruce shore sway, dawn rim light breathing

draw.subject: sockeye swimming through the weir camera lane
draw.action: cream odometer digits roll +1 +2 +3 as each passes; the raven perches on the rail keeping its own scratch tally, unimpressed
draw.annotation: SITKA TRIBE OF ALASKA: their river, their count

beats.means: self-determination as infrastructure, with the honest-tally gag paying off
beats.shows: the weir lane odometer rolling as sockeye pass, the raven's scratch tally

choreo.primary: cream odometer digits roll +1 +2 +3 as each sockeye crosses the lane
choreo.reaction: the raven adds a scratch mark after each click, head-tilting at the machine
choreo.ambient: salmon queue undulates, water sparkle, rail grain highlights shift

draw.subject: the fogged lens from the cold open
draw.action: the same chrome salmon noses up; the odometer flips one more digit; the caption question resolves
draw.annotation: WHO COUNTS THIS FISH? ... YOU DO.

beats.means: loop closed: the question got an answer
beats.shows: the fogged lens again, the final digit flip, the resolving caption

choreo.primary: the odometer flips one final digit as the caption resolves to YOU DO.
choreo.reaction: the salmon holds its gaze at the lens a beat then swims on
choreo.ambient: silt motes drift, caustics shimmer, the glass fog slowly re-blooms

shots.centered: the salmon nose and the flipping cream digit at frame center

hero.name: chrome salmon at the lens
hero.detail_note: salmon body S-curve, pectoral and tail fins, wary eye, fogged lens ring with condensation drips, brass odometer chip with rolling cream digit, drifting silt motes

shots.centered: the thin braided channel between the two shouting banks
shots.means: the lens pulls back to reveal the argument the fish lives inside

hero.name: the dueling banks diorama
hero.detail_note: left bank family (flannel, vest, worried faces), right bank crew and boat, jagged speech shards, two tilted mismatched StatCards, raven mid-decimal-heist, gravel bar ribcage, gaunt channel, far ridge in atmosphere

shots.centered: the fishing boat riding the swell above the pressed-down run curve
shots.means: travel to the fleet's world and slice it honest: deck above, ocean forces below

hero.name: FishingBoat on the honest ocean
hero.detail_note: sheer-bow hull with waterline stripe, wheelhouse windows, mast and rigging, net drum with the flung salmon arc, splash on the neighbor, cross-section waterline, cold swell mass, red run-curve bending down

shots.centered: the counting room: the frame-wall grid, the towers, the lineup, the ratcheting clicker wheel
shots.means: a paper sheet wipes us into the counting room where the bottleneck breaks

hero.name: the counting machine at work
hero.detail_note: buried observer with pencil, paper storm sheets, calendar shedding pages, grid wall of net-cam frames, pollock tower through ceiling, salmon pile with flag, brass counting head with cream dial, clock spinning to HOURS, lineup fish with expressions, STILL RESEARCH stamp

shots.camera: static:a trembling pen over a document reads strongest locked; all motion lives in the pen tremble and the stamp
shots.stage3d: flat:full-frame document close-up, depth adds nothing to a flat page
shots.centered: the pen tip hovering over the bill title
shots.means: one calendar page from the paper storm lands and becomes the bill

hero.name: the bill and the trembling pen
hero.detail_note: document with ruled lines and title block, hovering pen with red grip band, TRANSPARENCY stamp, wax-paper texture, drop shadow

shots.centered: the referee small and centered on the dry gravel bar, clicker held high
shots.means: the stamp's thud becomes the whistle blast; the camera rises from the settled fight to the empty truth

hero.name: the referee on the dry bar
hero.detail_note: striped vest with ink outlines, whistle with lanyard, raised brass clicker with cream dial, ankle-deep waterline ring, long raking dawn shadow, gravel ribcage bars receding, at most 3 fish flecks in the channel

shots.centered: the timber weir lane and its rolling odometer, biologist standing at her stake
shots.means: the clicker's circular dial graphic-matches to the weir's circular odometer housing: the count comes home

hero.name: the Redoubt Lake video weir
hero.detail_note: timber legs with grain and pegs, camera housing with lens ring, brass odometer chip with rolling cream digits, biologist in tribal vest planting the stake, sockeye passing in the lane, raven scratch-tally on the rail, water shimmer band, spruce shore, warm dawn rim

hook.frame1: extreme close: a chrome salmon nose against a fogged underwater lens, a brass chip's cream digit mid-flip to 0001, silt motes drifting, caption WHO COUNTS THIS FISH?
hook.headline: WHO COUNTS THIS FISH?
hook.loopback: the same fogged lens returns at the button; the odometer flips once more and the caption question resolves to YOU DO.

audio_arc.notes: riser at 40.0 must fully resolve to hollow wind with zero residual music before the 45.0 payoff swell; impact SFX land +2 to +5 frames after their visual event; the STILL RESEARCH die stamp (28.5) and TRANSPARENCY ink pad (33.0) use distinct timbres

reveals.what: pollock frame-tower builds through the ceiling beside the modest salmon pile; the clock lands on HOURS
boom-up reveals the referee tiny on a dry gravel bar over a near-empty river (at most 3 fish visible)
odometer digits roll upward from the weir chip anchor as each sockeye passes
